account.ask-have: Don't already have an account on our beautiful platform?

delete.confirm: If you want to delete your account and all data associated with it, press the delete button:
delete.confirm-twice: By confirming the deletion, you will permanently and irrevocably delete your account from our database and will loose your access to our easy management interface for your domains.
delete.confirm-password: To ensure this is really you, please enter your password:
delete.consequence: Your domains owned on others platforms will not be affected by the deletion, they'll continue to respond with the current dataset.
delete.remain-data: For technical reason, your account will be deleted right after your validation, but some data from your account will persist until the next database clean up.
delete.success: Your account have been successfully deleted. We hope to see you back soon.

account.no-password-change: You are identified through an external service, if you need to change your password, change it on the dedicated interface of {{provider}}.

signup.join-call: Join our nice platform in less than 2 minutes!
signup.address-why: We'll use your address to {{identify}} you on this platform, and to contact you for {{security-operations}}.
signup.receive-update: Keep me informed of future big improvements
signup.login-now: You can now login with your credentials.

common.add-object: Add a {{thing}} object to this service
common.survey: A remark? A comment to share? Don't hesitate to write to us!

domains.title: Your domains
domains.description: Browse and manage all your domain names in one place

alert.remove: This action only removes {{domain}} from your happyDomain managed domains. All history and abstracted zones will be discarded. The domain {{domain}} remains fully intact at the provider. Are you sure you want to continue?

domains.and-more-filtered: {{count:eq; 0:no more filtered domain; 1:1 more filtered domain; default:{{count}} more filtered domains}}
domains.added-success: Great! {{domain}} has been added. You can manage it right now.

apply.double-check: You see this confirmation dialog because you choose in your preference to always confirm before applying corrections.
apply.nochange: There is no changes to apply! Current zone is in sync with the server.
apply.change-already-applied: Changes you requested seems to be already applied.
apply.others: {{count:eq; 0:no other change; 1:{{count}} other change; default:{{count}} others changes}}
apply.prepare-info: The provider will execute {{nbDiffs}} correction(s) for your {{nbSelected}} selected change(s):
apply.prepare-warning: The number of corrections differs from your selection. Please review before confirming.

domains.give-explicit-name: Give an explicit name in order to easily find this service.
domains.filtered-no-result: There is no domain with those filters.

view.description: Review the modifications that will be applied to {{domain}}

view.subtitle: Your zone in standard BIND format

instruction.bad-link: The link you followed is invalid. Check that you copied the entire link and retry.
instruction.check-inbox: Please check your inbox in order to validate your e-mail address.
instruction.new-confirmation: If you need a new confirmation e-mail, just enter your address in the form below.
instruction.validate-address: In order to validate your e-mail address, please check your inbox, and follow the link contained in the message.
instruction.validated: Your new e-mail address is now validated!

email.send-recover: Send me an e-mail to recover my account
email.recover: In order to recover your account, we'll send you an e-mail containing a link that will allow you to redefine your password

validation-unavailable.title: Email Validation Unavailable

captcha.human-check: Are you a human? Please check the box below to continue:

domaininfo.page-description: Look up registration information for any domain name

domaininfo.domain-not-found: This domain name does not appear to be registered.

status-descriptions.ok: The domain is active with no restrictions.
status-descriptions.clientTransferProhibited: Transfer to another registrar is locked.
status-descriptions.serverTransferProhibited: Transfer is prohibited by the registry.
status-descriptions.serverUpdateProhibited: Domain updates are prohibited by the registry.
status-descriptions.serverDeleteProhibited: Domain deletion is prohibited by the registry.
status-descriptions.clientHold: The domain is on hold (not resolving).
status-descriptions.serverHold: The domain is on hold by the registry.
status-descriptions.pendingTransfer: A transfer to another registrar is pending.

errors.password-change: Unable to change your password account

errors.account-no-auth: You're using happyDomain without authentication. You cannot manage other account properties.
errors.domain-all-imported: This account on {{provider}} doesn't have any more domain to import.

logs.no-entry: No entry in the domain log.

onboarding.intro: Let's set up your account and start managing your domains. Follow these simple steps to get started.

beta.part1: happyDomain is functional but still very much a work in progress.  If something doesn't work, please
beta.part2: . We're highly responsive and each reported bug helps us improve the tool for everyone. Thanks in advance 🙏

welcome.purpose: {{happyDomain}} makes domain management simple and intuitive. Whether you have one domain or hundreds, our platform gives you a centralized way to manage them all.
welcome.use: Use {{happyDomain}} as a replacement interface to your usual domain name provider. It'll still rely on your provider's infrastructure, via our simple interface.

unified.title: Unified Management
unified.description: Manage all your domains in one place, regardless of where they're registered.

connect.title: Connect Your First Domain Provider
connect.intro: Connect your domain registrar or DNS provider to import your domains automatically. We support most major providers.
connect.noprovider: Can't find your provider in this list?
connect.nodomain: I don't own any domain yet

import.title: Import Your Domains
import.intro: Select the domains you want to manage with happyDomain.

explore.bravo: {{count:eq; 1:Your domain is; default:Your domains are}} now properly configured and ready to use.
explore.title: Explore happyDomain Features
explore.intro: Now that your domain is set up, discover all the powerful features happyDomain has to offer:

history.title: Record History
history.description: Track all changes made to your DNS records with version history and rollback options.

api.title: API Access
api.description: Automate domain management with our comprehensive API for developers and teams.

monitoring.title: Monitoring
monitoring.description: Real-time monitoring of your domains with alerts for any issues or expirations.

security.title: Security Check
security.description: Automated security audits to ensure your domain configurations follow best practices.

no-sale.title: You need to own a domain to use happyDomain.
no-sale.description: {{happyDomain}} does not sell domains yet. To start using our interface, you need to buy a domain from one of our supported providers.
no-sale.buy-advice: We'll provide some guidance in the near future on how to easily buy a domain name. So stay tuned and get in touch with us if you can help us build a comprehensive guide.

password.confirm-description: Re-enter your new password to confirm
password.current-description: Enter your current password to verify your identity
password.new-description: Choose a strong password with at least 8 characters, including numbers, lowercase and uppercase letters
password.fill: In order to recover your account, please fill the following form, with a fresh password.

recovery-unavailable.title: Password Recovery Unavailable
recovery-unavailable.description: The email functionality has been disabled on this server. Please contact your administrator to reset your password.

password.success: You can now login with your new password.
password.success-change: Your account's password has been changed with success.

CAA.all-issuers-body: With those parameters, all issuer can create certificate for this domain and subdomain.
CAA.all-wild-issuers-body: With those parameters, all issuer can create wildcard certificate for this domain and subdomain.
CAA.incident-response-text: How would you want to be contacted in case of violation of the current security policy?
CAA.mail-all-allowed-title: No CA constraint for S/MIME certificates issuance.
CAA.mail-all-allowed-body: All CA can emit S/MIME certificates for your domain. You should disallow or restrict to certain CA. S/MIME certificates are used for example to sign and encrypt e-mail communications. If you do not use such certificate, you should disallow their issuance.
CAA.no-issuers-body: With those parameters, no issuer is allowed to create certificate for this subdomain.
CAA.no-mail-title: No issuer authorized for S/MIME certificate.
CAA.no-mail-body: With those parameters, no issuer is authorized to create S/MIME certificate for this domain and subdomain.
CAA.no-wild-body: With those parameters, no issuer is authorized to create wildcard certificate for this domain and subdomain. But this can be override with the following settings:
CAA.title: Certificates issuance
CAA.wild-same-body: With those parameters, all issuer authorized for certificate issuance can also create wildcard certificate for this domain and subdomain.
CAA.vmc-all-allowed-title: No CA constraint for VMC certificates issuance.
CAA.vmc-all-allowed-body: All CA can emit VMC certificates for your domain. VMC certificates are used to display verified logos in email clients. If you do not use such certificate, you should disallow their issuance.
CAA.no-vmc-title: No issuer authorized for VMC certificate.
CAA.no-vmc-body: With those parameters, no issuer is authorized to create VMC certificate for this domain and subdomain.
CAA.contact-info-text: Provide contact details for certificate-related communications.

service.new-description: Click here to add a new service to this subdomain.
service.ttl-tip: 0 means default TTL, others values are exprimed in seconds

sessions.usage: Here is an example on how to use the generated secret:

applyconfirm.description: Choose when to show a confirmation step before applying changes to your DNS provider

zoneview.title: Zone view layout
zoneview.description: Choose how you want to display your domain zones

resolver.custom-description: Enter the addresse (a domain name or an IP) of the resolver you want to use.
resolver.domain-description: Indicate the domain you search the records. For example, you can try {{domain}}.
resolver.error-description: The DNS query could not be completed due to an error.
resolver.field-description: What kind of DNS record you want to see. For example: A is for IPv4, AAAA is for IPv6, ...
resolver.no-answer: The DNS query completed successfully, but no records were found for this domain and type.
resolver.page-description: Query DNS records for any domain
resolver.resolver-description: This is the server we will ask for the information.
resolver.results-description-default: DNS records found for your query
resolver.showDNSSEC: Show DNSSEC records in answer (if any)

generator.title: DNS Record Generators
generator.description: Free online DNS record generators: CAA, DMARC, SPF, MX, DKIM, SRV, and more. Build and preview DNS zone records instantly.
generator.subtitle: Pick a record type to build and preview its DNS zone entry — no account needed.

svctype.title: {{name}} Generator
svctype.description: Free online {{name}} DNS record generator. Configure and preview your {{name}} record in zone file format.
svctype.domain-help: Enter the domain name where the record will be created. The generated records will use this domain.
svctype.fill-form: Fill in the form above to generate records.
svctype.cta-text: happyDomain lets you manage all your DNS records through a visual interface — no zone file editing required.

zones.retrieve-subtitle: Fetching DNS records from your provider

run-check.title: Run Check
run-check.configure-info: Configure checker options below. Pre-filled values are from domain-level settings.
run-check.no-options: This checker has no configurable options. Click "Run Check" to execute with default settings.
run-check.no-run-options: This checker has no run-time options. You can still override advanced settings below.

list.title: Checks for
list.no-checks: No checks available for this domain.
list.prometheus-metrics-copied: Prometheus metrics URL copied to clipboard
list.prometheus-metrics-copy-failed: Failed to copy Prometheus metrics URL: {{error}}

other-checkers.title: Other available checkers
other-checkers.description: These checkers are not directly associated with this domain but can be configured with domain-specific options.

children-checkers.title: Zone & service checkers
children-checkers.description: These checkers apply to zones or individual services. You can configure them here, but results will only appear when running checks at the appropriate scope.

schedule.interval-hint: Between {{intervalMin}} and {{intervalMax}}. Default: {{intervalDefault}}. The check will run once per interval.
schedule.no-schedule-yet: No schedule created yet. Save to create one.

executions.no-results: No check results yet. Click "Run Check Now" to execute the check.
executions.title: Check Executions ({{count}})
executions.delete-all-confirm: Are you sure you want to delete ALL check results for this checker? This cannot be undone.
executions.deleted-all: All check results have been deleted.

result.no-results: No results available for this execution.

checkers.title: Checkers
checkers.description: Configure automated checks for your domains

detail.name: Name:
detail.no-configurable-options: This checker has no configurable options.

missing-selector.title: Missing selector
missing-selector.detail: DKIM records must be published as <selector>._domainkey.<domain>.

invalid-selector.title: Invalid selector "{{selector}}"
invalid-selector.detail: Selectors must contain only letters, digits, hyphens or underscores.

parse-error.title: Could not parse the DKIM record
parse-error.detail: The TXT value does not look like a key=value DKIM record.

missing-key.title: Missing public key (p=)
missing-key.detail: Every DKIM record must publish a key. Use an empty p= only to revoke a key.

revoked-key.title: Empty key, this DKIM record is revoked
revoked-key.detail: An empty p= tag tells verifiers to ignore this selector.

invalid-base64.title: Public key is not valid base64
invalid-base64.detail: p= must contain a base64-encoded SubjectPublicKeyInfo.

weak-rsa-key.title: RSA key too short
weak-rsa-key.detail: RFC 8301 forbids RSA keys shorter than 1024 bits.

short-rsa-key.title: RSA key shorter than recommended
short-rsa-key.detail: Use 2048-bit (or longer) RSA keys per RFC 8301 §3.2.

deprecated-hash.title: Deprecated hash algorithm "{{hash}}"
deprecated-hash.detail: RFC 8301 forbids SHA-1 in DKIM signatures.

unknown-flag.title: Unknown flag "{{flag}}"
unknown-flag.detail: RFC 6376 defines only "y" (testing) and "s" (strict i= alignment).

testing-mode.title: Testing mode is enabled (t=y)
testing-mode.detail: Verifiers may ignore signatures from this selector. Remove the y flag once deployed.

deprecated-granularity.title: g= tag is deprecated
deprecated-granularity.detail: Granularity was removed in RFC 6376; modern verifiers ignore it.

wrong-owner-name.title: DMARC record is not at _dmarc.{{name}}
wrong-owner-name.detail: DMARC records must be published at _dmarc.<domain>.

parse-error.title: Could not parse the DMARC record
parse-error.detail: The TXT value does not look like a key=value DMARC record.

missing-version.title: Missing v=DMARC1
missing-version.detail: A DMARC record must start with the version tag v=DMARC1.

missing-policy.title: Missing policy (p=)
missing-policy.detail: DMARC requires a policy: none, quarantine or reject.

invalid-policy.title: Unknown policy "{{policy}}"
invalid-policy.detail: p= must be one of: none, quarantine, reject.

invalid-sp.title: Unknown subdomain policy "{{policy}}"
invalid-sp.detail: sp= must be one of: none, quarantine, reject.

monitoring-only.title: Policy is p=none (monitoring only)
monitoring-only.detail: Receivers will collect reports but will not act on failed messages. Move to quarantine or reject once you trust the reports.

invalid-pct.title: pct="{{pct}}" is out of range
invalid-pct.detail: pct must be an integer between 0 and 100.

partial-deployment.title: DMARC applied to {{pct}}% of mail
partial-deployment.detail: Only a fraction of failing messages will be subject to the DMARC policy. Use this during rollout, then remove pct= once you trust the policy.

invalid-ri.title: ri="{{ri}}" is invalid
invalid-ri.detail: ri must be a positive integer in seconds (default 86400).

invalid-fo.title: Unknown failure-reporting option "{{value}}"
invalid-fo.detail: fo= accepts 0, 1, d or s (or a colon-separated combination).

unknown-rf.title: Unknown report format "{{value}}"
unknown-rf.detail: Only afrf is defined by RFC 7489.

empty-uri.title: Empty {{tag}} entry
empty-uri.detail: Remove the empty value or replace it with a destination URI.

invalid-uri-scheme.title: {{tag}} URI "{{uri}}" is not mailto/http
invalid-uri-scheme.detail: DMARC report URIs must use mailto: (recommended) or http(s):.

invalid-mailto.title: Malformed mailto URI in {{tag}}: "{{uri}}"
invalid-mailto.detail: Expected mailto:user@example.com (an optional !size suffix is allowed).

no-alignment-source.title: No DKIM or SPF record found in this zone
no-alignment-source.detail: DMARC needs at least one aligned mechanism. Without a DKIM or SPF record, no message can pass DMARC.

no-alignment-source-enforcing.title: p={{policy}} but no DKIM or SPF record in this zone
no-alignment-source-enforcing.detail: Every legitimate message will fail DMARC and be quarantined or rejected. Publish DKIM and/or SPF before enforcing.

strict-dkim-no-record.title: adkim=s but no DKIM record in this zone
strict-dkim-no-record.detail: Strict DKIM alignment requires the d= signing domain to match exactly. With no DKIM published, only SPF alignment can succeed.

wrong-owner-name.title: MTA-STS record is not at _mta-sts.{{name}}
wrong-owner-name.detail: MTA-STS records must be published at _mta-sts.<domain>.

parse-error.title: Could not parse the MTA-STS record
parse-error.detail: The TXT value does not look like a key=value MTA-STS record.

missing-version.title: Missing v=STSv1
missing-version.detail: An MTA-STS TXT record must start with v=STSv1.

missing-id.title: Missing policy id (id=)
missing-id.detail: MTA-STS requires a policy identifier; bump it whenever the policy file changes.

invalid-id.title: Invalid policy id "{{id}}"
invalid-id.detail: id must be 1 to 32 alphanumeric characters per RFC 8461 sec. 3.1.

policy-dns-error.title: Cannot resolve mta-sts host
policy-dns-error.detail: DNS lookup for the mta-sts.<domain> hostname failed. Publish an A/AAAA record so the policy at {{url}} can be reached.

policy-tls-error.title: TLS handshake failed for the policy host

policy-not-found.title: No policy file at {{url}}
policy-not-found.detail: RFC 8461 sec. 3.3: the policy MUST be served at this URL. Publish the file or remove the TXT record.

policy-http-error.title: Policy fetch returned HTTP {{code}}
policy-http-error.detail: The policy host responded with a non-2xx status; receivers will treat the policy as missing.

policy-redirect.title: Policy host attempted a redirect (HTTP {{code}})
policy-redirect.detail: RFC 8461 sec. 3.3 forbids redirects on the policy URL. Serve the file directly at {{url}}.

policy-too-large.title: Policy body is too large
policy-too-large.detail: The file at {{url}} exceeds the size cap (64 KiB). MTA-STS policies are very small; trim the file.

policy-missing-version.title: Policy file is missing the version key
policy-missing-version.detail: Add a 'version: STSv1' line at the top of {{url}}.

policy-missing-mode.title: Policy file is missing the mode key
policy-missing-mode.detail: Add a 'mode: enforce' (or testing / none) line in {{url}}.

policy-invalid-mode.title: Policy mode "{{mode}}" is unknown
policy-invalid-mode.detail: Mode must be one of: enforce, testing, none.

policy-mode-none.title: Policy mode is "none" (MTA-STS effectively disabled)
policy-mode-none.detail: Receivers will treat this as if no policy were published. Switch to enforce once you trust the policy.

policy-mode-testing.title: Policy mode is "testing"
policy-mode-testing.detail: Receivers will report failures but still deliver. Switch to enforce once reports are clean.

policy-missing-mx.title: Policy is missing mx entries
policy-missing-mx.detail: A {{mode}} policy must list at least one mx pattern; otherwise no mail can be delivered.

policy-missing-max-age.title: Policy file is missing max_age
policy-missing-max-age.detail: Add a 'max_age: <seconds>' line. Common values are 86400 (1 day) to 604800 (1 week) during rollout, then larger.

policy-invalid-max-age.title: Policy max_age "{{maxAge}}" is out of range
policy-invalid-max-age.detail: max_age must be between 0 and 31557600 seconds (1 year).

policy-short-max-age.title: Policy max_age is short ({{maxAge}}s)
policy-short-max-age.detail: RFC 8461 recommends at least one week. Short values defeat caching and force receivers to refetch often.

null-mx-with-others.title: Null MX cannot coexist with other MX records
null-mx-with-others.detail: RFC 7505 requires that a null MX (target ".") be the only MX record in the set.

null-mx-non-zero-preference.title: Null MX preference should be 0 (got {{preference}})
null-mx-non-zero-preference.detail: RFC 7505 specifies preference 0 for the null MX record.

invalid-target.title: Invalid MX target "{{target}}"
invalid-target.detail: MX targets must be valid hostnames per RFC 1035.

invalid-preference.title: MX preference "{{preference}}" is out of range
invalid-preference.detail: MX preference must be a 16-bit unsigned integer (0..65535).

duplicate-target.title: Duplicate MX target "{{target}}"
duplicate-target.detail: The same target appears more than once. Keep only one record.

target-is-cname.title: MX target "{{target}}" is a CNAME
target-is-cname.detail: RFC 5321 sec. 5.1 forbids MX records from pointing to a CNAME.

target-no-address.title: MX target "{{target}}" has no A/AAAA in this zone
target-no-address.detail: Add a Server entry for this name, or check that the target FQDN is correct.

wrong-owner-name.title: TLS-RPT record is not at _smtp._tls.{{name}}
wrong-owner-name.detail: TLS-RPT records must be published at _smtp._tls.<domain>.

parse-error.title: Could not parse the TLS-RPT record
parse-error.detail: The TXT value does not look like a key=value TLS-RPT record.

missing-version.title: Missing v=TLSRPTv1
missing-version.detail: A TLS-RPT TXT record must start with v=TLSRPTv1.

missing-rua.title: Missing rua= report destination
missing-rua.detail: TLS-RPT requires at least one rua URI to receive aggregate reports.

empty-rua.title: Empty rua entry
empty-rua.detail: Remove the empty value or replace it with a destination URI.

invalid-rua-scheme.title: rua URI "{{uri}}" is not mailto/http
invalid-rua-scheme.detail: TLS-RPT report URIs must use mailto: or http(s):.

missing-version.title: Missing v=spf1 prefix
missing-version.detail: An SPF record must start with the version tag v=spf1.

wrong-version.title: Unsupported SPF version: {{version}}
wrong-version.detail: Only v=spf1 is defined. Other versions are unsupported and will be ignored by receivers.

no-all-mechanism.title: No 'all' mechanism nor redirect
no-all-mechanism.detail: Without a terminating 'all' mechanism (or a redirect modifier), receivers fall back to a neutral result for unknown senders. Add e.g. -all or ~all at the end.

multiple-all.title: Multiple 'all' mechanisms ({{count}})
multiple-all.detail: Only one 'all' mechanism is allowed; subsequent ones are ignored.

all-not-last.title: 'all' mechanism is not the last term
all-not-last.detail: Mechanisms after 'all' are unreachable. Move 'all' to the end of the record.

redirect-with-all.title: redirect= modifier combined with an 'all' mechanism
redirect-with-all.detail: When 'all' is present, the redirect modifier is ignored. Remove one of them.

multiple-redirect.title: Multiple redirect= modifiers
multiple-redirect.detail: Only one redirect modifier is allowed per SPF record.

ptr-deprecated.title: ptr mechanism is deprecated
ptr-deprecated.detail: The 'ptr' mechanism is slow and deprecated by RFC 7208. Use ip4/ip6 or include instead.

too-many-lookups.title: More than {{max}} DNS lookups ({{count}})
too-many-lookups.detail: RFC 7208 sec. 4.6.4: an SPF record must not require more than 10 DNS lookups. Counting include, a, mx, exists, ptr and redirect locally, this record already exceeds that budget.

many-lookups.title: Approaching the SPF lookup limit ({{count}}/{{max}})
many-lookups.detail: Counting include, a, mx, exists, ptr and redirect locally; recursion may push you above the 10-lookup hard limit.

mechanism-missing-value.title: Mechanism '{{mechanism}}' requires a value
mechanism-missing-value.detail: include, exists and redirect must be followed by a domain name (e.g. include:_spf.example.com).

duplicate-mechanism.title: Duplicate term: {{mechanism}}
duplicate-mechanism.detail: Repeating the same mechanism has no effect and bloats the record.

empty-term.title: Empty SPF term
empty-term.detail: Remove blank entries; a stray space produces an empty directive.

unknown-mechanism.title: Unknown SPF mechanism '{{mechanism}}'
unknown-mechanism.detail: RFC 7208 sec. 5 defines all, include, a, mx, ptr, ip4, ip6 and exists. Anything else is treated as a permerror by verifiers.

unknown-modifier.title: Unknown SPF modifier '{{modifier}}='
unknown-modifier.detail: Only redirect= and exp= are defined by the SPF RFCs. Verifiers ignore unknown modifiers, but this is usually a typo.

length-exceeds-txt-string.title: Record length is {{length}} characters
length-exceeds-txt-string.detail: A single TXT string is capped at 255 characters; longer records must be split into multiple strings within the same RR.

recursive-too-many-lookups.title: Recursive SPF walk exceeds the {{max}}-lookup limit ({{count}})
recursive-too-many-lookups.detail: After resolving every include and redirect, this record needs more than 10 DNS lookups. Receivers will return PermError instead of evaluating it.

recursive-many-lookups.title: Recursive SPF walk uses {{count}}/{{max}} lookups
recursive-many-lookups.detail: You are within the budget but close to the limit. Adding another include or expanding an existing one is likely to push you over.

too-many-void-lookups.title: More than {{max}} void DNS lookups ({{count}})
too-many-void-lookups.detail: RFC 7208 §4.6.4 forbids more than two NXDOMAIN/NoData responses while evaluating SPF; receivers will return PermError.

include-loop.title: SPF include loop on {{domain}}
include-loop.detail: An include or redirect points back to a domain already evaluated in the chain. Break the cycle.

include-no-spf.title: No SPF record at {{domain}}
include-no-spf.detail: The mechanism {{mechanism}} resolves to a domain that does not publish v=spf1. The lookup still counts against the budget.

include-resolver-error.title: Could not check {{domain}}
include-resolver-error.detail: The resolver hit a transient error or a timeout while walking {{mechanism}}. Try again later.

include-error.title: Issue resolving {{domain}}
include-error.detail: The mechanism {{mechanism}} could not be fully evaluated.
